decisions: design_reference: blue header + menu grid + ReservationForm

proposals.source_quote: All endpoints should use the /api/v1/ prefix for versioning
proposals.description: API route versioning convention using /api/v1/ prefix for all endpoints.

proposals.source_quote: Use Stripe for payment processing, it has the best developer experience

data.reason: Best developer experience for payment processing

proposals.description: Stripe selected as the payment processing provider.

proposals.source_quote: The product card should show image, title, price, and add-to-cart button

data.description: Displays product information vertically with image at top, followed by title, price, and add-to-cart button.

proposals.description: ProductCard component structure with image, title, price, and add-to-cart button elements.

proposals.source_quote: Also make sure the cart persists across page refreshes using localStorage

data.decision: Implement cart persistence using browser localStorage
data.rationale: Maintain cart state across page refreshes for better user experience

proposals.description: Cart state persisted to localStorage to survive page refreshes.

summary_4fields.project_summary: ShopWave is an e-commerce platform with API endpoints prefixed at /api/v1/, Stripe integration for payment processing, and a ProductCard component displaying image, title, price, and add-to-cart button. Cart state persists across page refreshes using localStorage.
user_facts: Prefers Stripe for payment processing due to developer experience
Values API versioning best practices
Interested in hiking (mentioned San Francisco trails)
key_facts: ProductCard component with image, title, price, add-to-cart

proposals.source_quote: Actually, let's switch to JWT instead of session-based auth, it's better for mobile clients

data.decision: Use JWT-based authentication instead of session-based auth

proposals.description: Switch from session-based to JWT authentication for improved mobile client support.

proposals.source_quote: Make sure we use refresh tokens with short-lived access tokens for security

data.decision: Implement refresh token pattern with short-lived access tokens
data.rationale: Enhanced security through token rotation and limited exposure window

proposals.description: Implement refresh token pattern with 15-minute expiring access tokens for security.

summary_4fields.project_summary: SecureVault's authentication approach was switched from session-based to JWT with a refresh token pattern. Access tokens will have a 15-minute expiration with separate refresh tokens for enhanced mobile client compatibility and security.
user_facts: Prefers JWT over session-based authentication for mobile applications
Values security best practices like refresh token patterns
pending_items: Implement JWT token generation and validation
Configure token expiration and rotation logic

proposals.source_quote: Make sure we use refresh tokens with short-lived access tokens for security

data.decision: Implement refresh token pattern with 15-minute access token expiration
data.rationale: Balances security with usability by using short-lived access tokens and refresh tokens

proposals.description: Access tokens expire in 15 minutes; refresh tokens enable long-lived sessions securely.

summary_4fields.project_summary: SecureVault's authentication approach was updated from session-based to JWT-based auth. The implementation will use refresh tokens paired with short-lived access tokens (15-minute expiration) to balance security and mobile client compatibility.

Values security practices like refresh token patterns and short-lived tokens

proposals.source_quote: Actually, let's switch to JWT instead of session-based auth, it's better for mobile clients

data.decision: Use JWT authentication with refresh token pattern
data.rationale: Better compatibility with mobile clients and improved security through token expiration

proposals.description: SecureVault will use JWT with refresh tokens instead of session-based authentication for mobile client compatibility.

proposals.source_quote: Make sure we use refresh tokens with short-lived access tokens for security

data.decision: Implement refresh token pattern with short-lived access tokens (15-minute expiration)
data.rationale: Reduces exposure window if access token is compromised while maintaining session continuity

proposals.description: Access tokens expire after 15 minutes, requiring refresh token exchange for continued authenticated requests.

summary_4fields.project_summary: SecureVault's authentication strategy was updated from session-based to JWT with a refresh token pattern. Short-lived access tokens (15-minute expiration) will be paired with refresh tokens for improved mobile compatibility and security.
user_facts: Prefers JWT over session-based authentication for mobile client support
Values security practices like token expiration and refresh token patterns
